verdict: Moderate risk of AI displacement, but some tasks may be automated

reasoning_steps.title: Routine cognition
reasoning_steps.body: Hydrologists perform tasks like data analysis, mapping, and modeling, which are increasingly automated by AI tools

reasoning_steps.title: AI capability gap
reasoning_steps.body: Current AI models can already analyze large datasets, predict water quality, and identify potential contaminants, but human judgment is still required for complex decisions

evidence_ledger.source: Felten et al. 2023 — AI Occupational Exposure scores

adjacent_careers.name: Environmental Scientist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both hydrologists and environmental scientists work with data to understand and mitigate the impact of human activities on the environment

adjacent_careers.name: Water Resources Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Hydrologists and water resources engineers often collaborate on projects involving water management and infrastructure development

recent_developments.headline: New AI model improves water quality prediction accuracy
recent_developments.impact: This development may lead to increased automation of tasks like data analysis and modeling in hydrology

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.
